_comment: Picker auto-resolve fingerprint catalog (#1762). Data-only — edited as data, never code. Each entry pairs a regex fingerprint with a resolution policy. The daemon's no-LLM detector (lib/bridge-picker.sh + lib/bridge-picker.py) matches the last ~40 captured pane lines against `match` (ALL regexes must be present), confirms `stuck_confirm_ticks` consecutive ticks with an unchanged pane hash, then dispatches `policy`. Install-local additions/overrides live in $BRIDGE_SHARED_DIR/picker-catalog.local.json (git-ignored, machine-specific OK there). Confidence: [approx] entries ship DISABLED until a verbatim pane capture replaces the regex (see the picker-resolve escalation skill); [exact] entries are grounded in a real observed string and may ship enabled. Catalog ground truth seeded by patch 2026-06-10 at the install's shared/picker-captures/INVENTORY.md.

entries.comment: Trust prompt. Strings verbatim from lib/bridge-tmux.sh bridge_tmux_claude_blocker_state_from_text ('Quick safety check:' + 'Yes, I trust this folder') — already grounded in-repo. policy=defer routes to the EXISTING trust machinery (recover_claude_bootstrap_blockers / bridge_tmux_prepare_claude_session); the picker stage does NOT key it directly. INVENTORY slug: claude-trust-folder.

Claude resume-from-summary blocker. Strings verbatim from lib/bridge-tmux.sh ('Resume from summary (recommended)' + 'Resume full session as-is') — grounded in-repo. policy=defer routes to the EXISTING summary-blocker machinery (recover_claude_bootstrap_blockers state=summary). NOTE: this is the resume-from-summary blocker, distinct from the resume/compact LIST picker (claude-resume-compact below).

TODO[verbatim-capture]: codex update prompt. Strings APPROX from INVENTORY.md #1 ('A new version of Codex is available' / 'Update now? [Y/n]'); the codex TUI may render a selectable list instead. Operator policy: Update + restart, then resume the SAME session (success = session_id preserved post-restart). DISABLED until shared/picker-captures/codex-update-<date>.txt lands and replaces these regexes. When enabling: keep expect_restart=true and post_resolve_verify=true so the restart-resume is confirmed, not assumed.

TODO[verbatim-capture]: claude resume / post-compact LIST picker. Strings APPROX from INVENTORY.md #2 ('Continue where you left off' / 'Start a new conversation'). Operator policy: pick CONTINUE-CURRENT ('그냥 시작' — continue the existing session). 'Start a new conversation' is the DESTRUCTIVE wrong answer (loses the thread) — encoded in destructive_match so the resolver refuses to advance if the highlighted/selected option matches it. DISABLED until shared/picker-captures/claude-resume-compact-<date>.txt lands. post_resolve_verify=true.

#1819. Claude session/usage-limit dialog shown when an interactive session hits its 5h window: header 'What do you want to do?' over the option pair '1. Stop and wait for limit to reset' / '2. Upgrade your plan'. Strings VERBATIM from the live 22-agent install pane capture in the issue (claude engine). Operator policy: ALWAYS option 1 (wait for the reset). select_first (Home -> option 1) + confirm mirrors claude-resume-compact's shape and lands the highlight on the wait option deterministically regardless of where the cursor starts. 'Upgrade your plan' is a BILLING action and must NEVER be auto-selected: it is encoded in destructive_match so the resolver's rail-(c) destructive-guard (bridge_picker_selected_is_destructive) REFUSES to advance and escalates instead if the upgrade option is the currently-selected (glyph-marked) line. The AND-pair of the two option strings is unique to this dialog, so it does not false-match other Claude pickers. post_resolve_verify=true: after resolution the session shows 'You've hit your session limit · resets <time>' and returns to an idle composer, which classifies clean.
match: Stop and wait for limit to reset

entries.comment: TODO[verbatim-capture]: codex login / session-expired auth surface. Strings APPROX from INVENTORY.md #4 ('Your session has expired' / 'Sign in again'). Operator policy: NEVER auto-key an auth flow — escalate only. policy=escalate means the detector files a queue task to the admin and sends ZERO keystrokes. Stays escalate-only forever (no keys field); enabling it only turns on detection->escalation, never auto-keying.

TODO[verbatim-capture]: claude tool-permission prompt (rare — fleet runs --dangerously-skip-permissions). Strings APPROX from INVENTORY.md #5 ('wants to run' / select Yes/No). Operator policy: NO blanket auto-yes — route to the install's permission-approval flow (patch-permission-approval skill / [PERMISSION] queue). policy=escalate sends ZERO keystrokes; escalation_route=permission_approval tags the task so the admin routes it correctly. Never gains a keys field.

NON-PICKER context signal — NOT a stuck state. Verbatim Claude CLI status string observed live 2026-06-10 ('Auto-update failed' + 'Run /doctor'). This is a non-blocking STATUS-LINE banner; agents keep taking prompts. policy=non_picker means the detector explicitly EXCLUDES it from stuck-state matching so it can never register as stuck or trigger a resolution. Kept in the catalog so the exclusion is data-driven and the precursor relationship to claude-update-manual (below) is documented.

PLACEHOLDER[verbatim-capture]: manual claude-update picker. As of 2026-06-10 claude auto-update is failing fleet-wide (see claude-autoupdate-failed), so a MANUAL update picker may soon appear. No real string observed yet — match is an obviously-unreachable sentinel so this entry can NEVER fire until a real capture replaces it. Documented disabled placeholder per the operator/patch heads-up. When the picker is seen: capture it to shared/picker-captures/claude-update-manual-<date>.txt, replace match with the verbatim regex, set the resume-preserving policy, and enable.

#1766 mitigation. Claude renders this blocking picker when a settings file cannot be read (e.g. an iso v2 agent EACCESing on its own controller-owned 0600 workdir/.claude/settings.json — the root cause #1766 fixes at the permission layer). Strings verbatim from the live cm-prod capture 2026-06-10: 'Settings Error' + 'Settings file could not be read' + the option 'Continue without these settings'. Operator policy: option '3. Continue without these settings' — the non-destructive, non-blocking choice (Claude proceeds without that one settings file; bridge-managed effective settings are reasserted on the next rerender). keys = select_first (Home -> option 1) then two 'down's (-> option 3) then confirm, so the highlight lands on option 3 deterministically regardless of where it starts. post_resolve_verify=true so a mismatched render does not blindly re-key. This is a SAFETY NET only — the real fix is the #1766 permission publish; the catalog keeps startup unblocked even if perms regress.
match: Settings file could not be read

entries.comment: #1783 fix. NON-PICKER context signal — the ORDINARY idle composer of Claude Code, not a stuck picker. The idle UI always renders the input-box border plus an EMPTY '❯' composer line and the 'bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)' / '? for shortcuts' footer; because the pane is static, the unknown-stuck tracker would otherwise escalate every idle session ~5min after the auto-resolve stage comes up (19/22 sessions live on v0.16.8). policy=non_picker is a HARD EXCLUSION so an idle composer can never register as unknown-stuck. The load-bearing piece is the EMPTY-composer regex '^❯\s*$': a real picker renders option text after the selector ('❯ 1. Yes, …') so it does NOT match this entry and still walks the unknown path. foreground_guard=true tail-scopes the exclusion (classify._idle_ready_tail_clear): it only short-circuits when this idle composer/footer is the LIVE TAIL of the capture with no foreground picker affordance at/below it — a real picker stacked as the foreground escalates, and stale menu-like scrollback ABOVE a tail idle composer is ignored. Strings verbatim from the issue's sanitized live capture (claude engine). Regex-engine dependency (#1800 dir.4): '^❯\s*$' clears because the composer's trailing glyph is NBSP and Python re '\s' matches NBSP under the default Unicode mode — if this catalog is ever evaluated by a non-Unicode-\s engine, widen to '^❯[\s\u00a0]*$'.
match: bypass permissions on \(shift\+tab to cycle\)|\? for shortcuts

entries.comment: #1783 fix. NON-PICKER context signal — the ORDINARY idle composer of the Codex TUI, not a stuck picker. policy=non_picker hard-excludes it so the static idle pane never escalates as unknown-stuck once the auto-resolve stage runs. TWO anchored fingerprints, both required. The composer regex matches ONLY an EMPTY composer — '(?m)^\s*[│┃|]?\s*›\s*(?:[│┃|]\s*)?$' = a line-leading '›' followed by only whitespace / a closing border. This is the P1-SAFE boundary (codex review #1783 rounds 8-9): a real picker OPTION row ALWAYS has text after '›' ('› Yes', '› /review current changes'), so it can NEVER match this fingerprint and is never hidden as idle. The deliberate trade-off: a Codex idle screen that renders GHOST placeholder text after '›' (e.g. '› Run /review …') is NOT caught by this entry and reaches the unknown path — but that residual is bounded by the 2-tick + 5-min unknown-stuck budget AND the per-pass storm fuse, which is strictly preferable to ever stranding a real stuck picker. The second regex '(?m)^.*·\s*~/' is the idle READY status footer (model/effort tokens then '· ~/<workdir>'). foreground_guard=true additionally tail-scopes the exclusion (classify._idle_ready_tail_clear): it only short-circuits when there is no foreground picker affordance below the idle composer line — stale menu-like scrollback ABOVE the idle composer is ignored. Strings grounded in the issue's sanitized live capture (codex engine).
